posts.author: gsapte
posts.text: There seems to be no evidence of worsening fundamentals to me. It looks more like Valuations being normalized from 60+ to 35. Long term Median P/E is also close to 35-37. That being said, sometimes when Market feels that, companies may not come up with New Bond Issues, and revenue of CRISIL may not grow more than 10%, it may not keep premium valuations intact and might reduce to its long term Mean. There is a possibility that, Inflation may go up, Interest rates and hence Bond Yields may go up and Companies might reduce new bonds/NCDs in next few quarters, and this could be one of the reasons…

posts.author: JJoy
posts.text: What is happening in CRISIL recently, why the stock prices are going down ?
